summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting primarily to discuss and approve a resolution of necessity for a 4.4 mill replacement fire and EMS levy to appear on the ballot. Chief Myers presented financial projections showing various mill rates and recommended 4.4 mills as a compromise that maintains staffing and safety while easing the tax burden on residents. The board also approved a $40,000 increase in fire apparatus maintenance appropriations due to unexpected vehicle repairs, and celebrated the start of a long-awaited environmental cleanup on Parker Road.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed unanimously
Motion to approve payment of bills for June 12–17, 2024 – passed unanimously
Motion to increase appropriations in repairs and maintenance (2111-220-323-0000) by $40,000 for fire apparatus repairs – passed unanimously
Resolution R-011-2024 approved: resolution of necessity to levy a 4.4 mill replacement tax for fire and emergency medical services – passed unanimously
action_items: Chief Myers to continue pursuing grants to offset capital cuts resulting from the 4.4 mill levy instead of 4.5
Chief Myers to continue progress on Station 18 pending capital bill approval
Board and community committee (Friends of the Fire Department) to develop a positive campaign strategy for the fire/EMS levy election
Director Meadows' team completed Gateway sign landscaping (acknowledged as done)
key_amounts: $40,000 increase in fire apparatus repairs and maintenance appropriations
$22,000 repair bill for 2015 engine rear end replacement
$10,000 in repairs for 2020 engines (brakes and related work)
4.4 mill replacement levy proposed (down from current 4.5 mills)
4.5 mill levy represents approximately 40% of the fire/EMS overall budget
$1.388 million projected shortfall after 5 years with a 4.5 mill renewal (not replacement)
$329,000 projected unencumbered funds after 5 years with 4.4 mill replacement
$564,000 projected unencumbered funds after 5 years with 4.5 mill replacement
$400,000–$500,000 needed in reserves to cover bills until first tax collection in April
$2 million recommended reserve by auditor's office (six months)
$86,627 projected unencumbered funds after 5 years with 4.3 mill replacement
~$600,000 projected deficit after 5 years with 4.0 mill replacement
notable_topics: Fire and EMS replacement levy discussion and resolution of necessity (4.4 mills)
Fire apparatus maintenance budget overrun due to catastrophic vehicle repairs
Ohio EPA Combs cleanup on Parker Road beginning after approximately 16 years
Station 18 progress update pending capital bill
Homestead exemption consideration in deciding not to combine existing levies
Fire staffing levels unchanged since 2003 despite anticipated township growth
notes: The fire/EMS levy previously failed at the last election; the board chose 4.4 mills as a compromise to ease tax burden while maintaining safety. Chief Myers explained that combining the three older levies (1982, 1996, 2002) with the current one would cause residents to lose their homestead exemption. The meeting was notably brief at approximately 16 minutes. A community presentation was held earlier in the evening before the formal meeting. Fire department staffing has remained the same since 2003 and future staffing increases may be considered in the next levy cycle.
title: BOT Special Meeting 6-17-24
